bead.title: agent: preclaim AC-quality scorer must count Test* function names and concrete CLI commands as verifiable
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The preclaim AC-quality scorer (event `kind:ac-quality-low`, `source:preclaim-ac-quality`) classifies AC lines that name a `Test*` function or a concrete CLI command as `kind:prose, verifiable:false`. This mis-scores beads that actually pass the 8-criterion authoring rubric, blocks them from autonomous execution, and gives operators a false signal that needs manual triage.

Observed example: `ddx-97375097` AC#1 names a specific JSON file path; AC#2 names a concrete `go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode … | rg …` command. Both were tagged `"kind":"prose","verifiable":false`. The scorer reports `verifiable=2/6` (only the trailing `cd cli && go test` and `lefthook run pre-commit` lines counted). The bead-authoring template (`docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md`) criterion (c) explicitly states the AC must "name a `Test*` function or a `go test -run` filter", so `Test*` function names are intended to count as verifiable.

ROOT CAUSE
- The classifier appears to recognize only `cd cli && go test …` and `lefthook run pre-commit` shapes as verifiable.
- Other strong verifiability signals are not counted:
  - Bare `Test[A-Z]\w+` function references (matches the rubric).
  - Concrete CLI command invocations: `go run …`, `go build …`, `bash scripts/…`, `bun run …`, etc.
  - File path with `:LINE` reference inside AC body.

ROOT CAUSE FILE:LINE (to be confirmed by implementer)
- Likely in `cli/internal/agent/` or `cli/internal/bead/` — search for `kind:ac-quality-low` and `preclaim-ac-quality` event emission. The classifier function maintains a regex set that needs extension.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend the classifier's verifiable-pattern set with:
  1. `\bTest[A-Z]\w+\b` — Test function references (mirror rubric language).
  2. Concrete command shapes: `\b(go run|go build|go test|bun run|bash scripts/|cd \S+ && )` (already partially present for `go test`/`cd cli`).
  3. File path with line ref: `\b[\w./-]+\.(go|md|yaml|json|sh|ts|tsx|svelte):\d+\b`.

A line matching any of these on its own merits `verifiable:true` regardless of other text. The scorer should then re-score `ddx-97375097`-shaped AC at >= 4/6.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- Whichever file in `cli/internal/agent/` or `cli/internal/bead/` defines the AC-quality classifier (find via `grep -rn 'preclaim-ac-quality\|ac-quality-low' cli/`).
- Test file alongside the classifier.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the rubric itself (`docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md`).
- Do not change how the AC-quality event is consumed downstream.
- Do not clear the existing `ac-quality:needs-refinement` labels on already-mis-scored beads — that's a separate housekeeping bead.

REFERENCES
- Helix align audit run-id: helix-align-20260518T074735Z (R5)
- Example mis-scored bead: ddx-97375097
bead.acceptance:
1. `grep -rn 'preclaim-ac-quality\|ac-quality-low' cli/` locates exactly one classifier implementation. Document the file in the commit message.
2. `TestPreClaimACQuality_TestFunctionNameCountsAsVerifiable` (new) constructs an AC line containing only `TestFoo_Bar_Baz` and asserts `verifiable=true` in the classifier output.
3. `TestPreClaimACQuality_ConcreteGoRunCommandCountsAsVerifiable` (new) constructs an AC line containing `go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode ./... | rg foo` and asserts `verifiable=true`.
4. `TestPreClaimACQuality_FilePathLineRefCountsAsVerifiable` (new) constructs an AC line containing `cli/cmd/work.go:133` and asserts `verifiable=true`.
5. `TestPreClaimACQuality_PlainProseStillNotVerifiable` (new) constructs an AC line "Operators should see better behavior in the queue." and asserts `verifiable=false`.
6. Replaying the classifier against the actual AC body of `ddx-97375097` (fixture: copy the body into a testdata file) returns `verifiable >= 4/6`.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run 'PreClaimACQuality|ACQuality' -count=1` green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
